Tomas de Berlanga (Santo Tomas), Ecuador - From your device
This is a Galápagos marine iguana (Amblyrhynchus cristatus), the only lizard in the world that forages in the sea.
They feed almost exclusively on marine algae found on shorelines and underwater.
To manage the high salt intake from their diet, they have specialized glands that allow them to "sneeze" out excess salt, often leaving a white crust on their heads.
The species is listed as Vulnerable due to climate events like El Niño, which reduce their food source and can cause them to temporarily shrink in size to survive.
